description: A composite enforcement card built around an inset close-up mugshot/portrait of a man (identified in the tweet as alleged MS-13 gang member Carlos Rivera-Alvarado of El Salvador). The inset is a tightly cropped, frontal photo of a man with short dark hair, light stubble and a stern expression, wearing a dark gray/green Philadelphia Eagles t-shirt (the team's bird logo and 'EAGLES' wordmark are visible), shot against a plain pale interior wall. This portrait is layered over a background photo of a quiet residential/commercial street scene: a low pale building, a wide empty street, sidewalk, parked cars, telephone/utility poles and lines, and trees under a clear blue sky, evoking the suburban New Jersey location (Vineland, N.J.) where the arrest occurred. The composition is a designed two-layer collage rather than a single candid snapshot, the hallmark of ICE's 'arrest announcement' card format. No agency seal or on-image headline text is present in this version; the only text is the incidental 'EAGLES' team branding on the subject's shirt. The messaging technique is the rogues-gallery/perp-spotlight: pairing a named, scowling suspect with the ordinary American neighborhood he was found in, to dramatize the threat and advertise a 'targeted enforcement' win. Target audience is the general public and ICE's social-media followers.
notable_text: (no designed headline; subject wears a Philadelphia 'EAGLES' t-shirt)
